title: The Future Is Domain-Specific Agents - Justin Schroeder, StandardAgents
summary: Justin Schroeder argues that domain-specific agents composed together represent a superior architecture to skills-based systems, enabling smaller models to perform real work and driving AI from internal tools to customer-facing products. The talk advocates for composition-over-inheritance as the foundational principle for useful AI systems.
writeup: In this AI Engineer talk, Justin Schroeder of StandardAgents makes the case that domain-specific agents (per-tool agents for Gmail, Sheets, and Notion, for example) composed together form the basis of useful AI systems. The argument: narrowing each agent's scope lets smaller models do real work while improving reliability and cost. The framing also positions this composed architecture as a path to customer-facing AI products rather than internal copilots alone, with skill composition and multi-agent design as the connecting concepts.
